text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

EXAMINATION: Computed tomography of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with intravenous contrast.

INDICATION: 48-year-old male with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia presenting with leukocytosis (WBC 84,000/uL), anemia, thrombocytopenia, and gingival hypertrophy. Pre-treatment staging examination to evaluate for extramedullary disease, organomegaly, lymphadenopathy, and end-organ involvement prior to initiation of induction chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast. No oral contrast was administered given urgency of the study. Axial images were obtained at 2.5 mm slice thickness with coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations. No prior imaging available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well aerated bilaterally without focal areas of consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or airspace disease. No pulmonary nodules or masses identified. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Pleura: No pleural effusions. No pleural thickening or pleural-based lesions.

Mediastinum: The mediastinum is midline. The heart is normal in size with no pericardial effusion. The aorta and great vessels demonstrate normal caliber and course without aneurysmal change. No mediastinal widening. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy; subcarinal, prevascular, right paratracheal, and aortopulmonary window nodal stations are all within normal size limits (all nodes measuring less than 8 mm in short axis).

Hila: No hilar lymphadenopathy bilaterally. No hilar masses.

Axillae: No axillary lymphadenopathy. Scattered small axillary nodes are present bilaterally, the largest measuring approximately 6 mm in short axis, within normal limits and without suspicious morphology.

Bones and Soft Tissues of the Chest: The thoracic skeleton demonstrates diffuse alteration of the medullary signal throughout the visualized vertebral bodies and posterior elements, as well as within the ribs and sternum. The medullary fat signal appears mildly heterogeneous and somewhat hyperdense relative to expected fatty marrow, most consistent with marrow replacement or congestion in the setting of the known leukemia diagnosis. No discrete lytic or blastic osseous lesions are identified. No cortical destruction.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is mildly enlarged, measuring approximately 17.2 cm in craniocaudal span in the right lobe on coronal reconstruction. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously in the portal venous phase without focal areas of hypo- or hyperenhancement to suggest discrete lesions, masses, or metastatic deposits. No hepatic cysts. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent with normal appearance. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without gallstones or wall thickening.

Spleen: The spleen is mildly enlarged, measuring approximately 14.1 cm in longest axis. The splenic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesions, nodules, or areas of infarction. Splenic vein is patent.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and signal throughout the head, neck, body, and tail without ductal dilatation, focal masses, or peripancreatic inflammatory changes. The main pancreatic duct is not dilated.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal in size and morphology. No adrenal nodules or masses.

Kidneys and Ureters: The kidneys are normal in size and symmetrical. There is bilateral symmetric cortical enhancement in the portal venous phase without focal cortical lesions, masses, or filling defects in the collecting systems. No hydronephrosis. No perinephric stranding. No renal calculi. The proximal ureters are not dilated.

Bowel: The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel appear unremarkable. No wall thickening, pneumatosis, or free intraperitoneal air. No bowel obstruction.

Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Paraaortic, paracaval, portocaval, and celiac nodal stations are within normal limits in size. No mesenteric lymphadenopathy. Multiple small mesenteric nodes are visualized but are not enlarged (all measuring less than 7 mm in short axis) and lack suspicious morphology.

Peritoneum and Mesentery: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or thickening to suggest peritoneal disease. Mesenteric fat is normal in attenuation.

Abdominal Aorta and Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber throughout. Celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric arteries arise normally.

Abdominal Osseous Structures: As noted in the chest, there is diffuse subtle increase in medullary density throughout the lumbar vertebral bodies and iliac wings, with heterogeneous replacement of normal fatty marrow signal, consistent with diffuse marrow infiltration in the setting of the known AML diagnosis. No discrete lytic lesions. No pathologic fractures. Cortical margins are intact throughout.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is well distended with homogeneous low-density urine. No wall thickening, mucosal irregularity, or intraluminal filling defects.

Male Reproductive Structures: The prostate gland is normal in size and contour for the patient's age. No seminal vesicle abnormality.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. External and internal iliac nodal stations are within normal limits bilaterally. No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Pelvic Vasculature: Iliac vessels are normal in caliber without aneurysmal change.

Pelvic Osseous Structures: The femoral heads, acetabulae, sacrum, and coccyx demonstrate the same pattern of diffusely abnormal medullary attenuation as described above. No discrete osseous lesions or cortical disruption. The sacroiliac joints appear normal.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Mild hepatomegaly (liver span 17.2 cm) and mild splenomegaly (spleen 14.1 cm in longest axis), without focal lesions in either organ. These findings are most consistent with systemic involvement of the patient's known acute myeloid leukemia and/or reactive changes in the setting of leukocytosis.

2. Diffuse heterogeneous medullary density throughout the visualized axial and appendicular skeleton, including the vertebral bodies, ribs, sternum, iliac wings, sacrum, and proximal femora. This pattern is consistent with diffuse marrow infiltration by leukemia and correlates with the markedly elevated peripheral blast count. No focal destructive lesions, lytic abnormalities, or cortical compromise.

3. No evidence of a chloroma (granulocytic sarcoma) or discrete extramedullary AML deposit anywhere in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. No soft tissue masses. No epidural or paraspinal soft tissue abnormality to suggest spinal cord compression.

4. No thoracic, mediastinal, hilar, axillary, retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

5. Lungs clear. No pleural effusion. No pericardial effusion.

6. No ascites or peritoneal disease.

7. Abdominal and pelvic vasculature unremarkable.

In summary, this pre-treatment staging CT demonstrates hepatosplenomegaly and diffuse osseous marrow abnormality consistent with systemic leukemic burden, but no discrete extramedullary disease, no chloroma, and no evidence of organ infiltration beyond the marrow and reticuloendothelial system. Findings are appropriate context for initiation of induction chemotherapy.
